topic.name: Tax Exclusions for Indebtedness
topic.description: Legislation regarding the exclusion from gross income of discharged principal residence indebtedness under the Internal Revenue Code.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend until January 1, 2019
exclusion from gross income of the discharge of qualified principal residence indebtedness
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2013
exclusion from gross income of income attributable to the discharge of indebtedness on a principal residence
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend until January 1, 2018
exclusion from gross income of the discharge of qualified principal residence indebtedness

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to amendments and coverage provisions under Medicare and Medicaid programs in the United States.

Document 1: Medicare Home Infusion Therapy Consolidated Coverage Act of 2007
consolidated coverage of home infusion therapy under Medicare part B
Document 2: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
temporary exemption from specified Medicare payment limits
Document 3: States Achieve Medicaid Expansion (SAME) Act of 2016
enhanced federal medical assistance percentage (FMAP) to every state that expands Medicaid coverage

topic.name: Education Reform Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at enhancing educational quality and access through community involvement, class size reduction, and online education standards.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to assist public elementary or secondary schools to function as full-service community schools
Requires such schools to participate in community-based efforts to coordinate and integrate educational, developmental, family, health, and other comprehensive services.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require recognized accrediting agencies to require distance education programs to have a secure login and passcode for Internet coursework.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to states or local educational agencies to reduce the size of core curriculum classes in public elementary and secondary schools
Requires grant funds to be used for constructing new classroom space, hiring additional teachers, and purchasing portable structures.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States pertaining to temporary duty suspensions and reductions on specific products.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's footwear.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: grant temporarily duty-free treatment of cyan 854 inkjet printing ink.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: reduce temporarily the duty on certain electric motor controllers.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Veterans Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving access to mental and emergency health care for veterans through amendments to existing laws and regulations.

Document 1: amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014
make a veteran eligible for mental health care at a non-Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) facility
Document 2: eliminates the requirement limiting reimbursement for emergency treatment in a non-Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) facility
requires the veteran to be an enrollee in the VA's patient enrollment system
Document 3: prohibits any Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) senior executive employee from being paid a bonus
VA shall report to Congress on the number and length of vacancies in VA medical center director positions

topic.name: Federal Environmental Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal regulations and policies aimed at managing and mitigating the environmental impacts of resource extraction and greenhouse gas emissions.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
carrying out any Act or program to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to revise the policy concerning resource development
include emphasis of the importance of protection, maintenance, and restoration of the marine and coastal environments.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas
establishes a civil penalty for noncompliance with this Act.

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Resettlement
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at reforming immigration policies, including refugee resettlement and adjustments for skilled workers.

Document 1: permits an alien applying for U.S. refugee admission who is referred for resettlement by the United Nations High Commissioner on Refugees (UNHCR)
establishes the Refugee Resettlement Fund in the Treasury
Document 2: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust to conditional permanent resident status up to 50,000 aliens who have earned a master's or doctorate degree in a science, technology, engineering, or mathematics field
eliminates the per-country numerical limitation for employment-based visas
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exclude from the definition of 'unauthorized alien'
consider an alien without lawful status to be an unauthorized alien whose employment is unlawful

topic.name: EMP Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on monitoring and reporting foreign countries' activities related to electromagnetic pulse weapons.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees
identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees
identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program

topic.name: Wilderness Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at designating and protecting wilderness areas and natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Designates specified national forest and Bureau of Land Management (BLM) public land in California as wilderness
Requires such areas to be administered by the Secretary of the Interior or Agriculture
Document 2: Establishes the Browns Canyon National Monument in Colorado to preserve the nationally significant biological, cultural, recreational, natural, wilderness, historic, scenic, riparian, geological, educational, and scientific values
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete a comprehensive management plan for the conservation and protection of the National Monument and the Wilderness
Document 3: Amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to adjust the boundaries of the North Cascades National Park and the Stephen Mather Wilderness
Prohibits anything in this Act from authorizing the sale or acquisition of any land or interest in land

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: Legislation and initiatives aimed at improving child welfare, preventing abuse, and protecting children from exploitation.

Document 1: Amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act (CAPTA) to revise the findings under such Act.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to study and report to Congress on shaken baby syndrome.
Document 2: Amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to enter into an agreement with the National Academy of Sciences to study and report to the Secretary and Congress on child welfare staff.
Allows funds from private nonprofit educational institutions to be considered as the state's share in claiming federal reimbursement for such expenditures.
Document 3: Amends the Missing Children's Assistance Act to require the Office of Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention's annual grant to the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children to be used to operate a cyber tipline.
Requires each state reporting on missing children to provide, with respect to each missing child report, a recent photograph of the child, if available.

topic.name: Support for Individuals with Disabilities
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving services, support, and care for individuals with disabilities, including autism spectrum disorders and veterans.

Document 1: establish the National Autism Spectrum Disorders Initiative
improve the lives of individuals with autism spectrum disorders through research
Document 2: make interactive training sessions available for family caregivers
provide family caregivers with information concerning public, private, and nonprofit agencies that offer support
Document 3: develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts
programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems who have served in the U.S. military

topic.name: Drug Pricing and Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating drug pricing and ensuring transparency in pharmaceutical agreements and practices.

Document 1: Amends the Clayton Act to make it unlawful for a person, in connection with the sale of a drug product, to be a party to any agreement resolving or settling a patent infringement claim

Requires the Chief Executive Officer or the company official responsible for negotiating any agreement to file a certification that materials filed with respect to such agreements are complete, final, and exclusive.
Document 2: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) to repeal the prohibition against Medicare participation by physician-owned hospitals
Restores or revives related previous law amended or repealed by PPACA as if PPACA had not been enacted.
Document 3: Requires manufacturers of certain drugs and biological products to report to the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) price increases that result in a 10% or more increase in the price of a drug over a 12-month period
Collected penalty funds must be used to carry out activities related to this reporting requirement and to improve consumer and provider information about drug value and drug price transparency.

topic.name: Ecosystem Restoration Projects
topic.description: The documents discuss various federal acts and initiatives aimed at restoring and enhancing ecosystems through water management and habitat restoration efforts.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality
in accordance with the recommendations of the report of the Chief of Engineers
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior
to enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem
Document 3: Directs the Chief of Engineers, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration
identify demonstration projects for construction and restoration of the San Gabriel River Watershed

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws and regulations aimed at protecting consumers in the mortgage lending process and ensuring fair practices in mortgage origination and loan modification services.

Document 1: Mortgage Foreclosure Rescue and Loan Modification Services Fraud Prevention Act of 2009
prohibits or restricts mortgage foreclosure rescue and loan modification services from requesting or receiving any funds until any such services have been fully performed
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to prescribe fiduciary standards for originators of residential mortgages
Prohibits steering incentives in connection with mortgage loan origination
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to the prohibitions against: steering incentives
Allows a mortgage originator, at the consumer's request, to reduce the amount of compensation the originator is receiving

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation and Management
topic.description: Legislation and initiatives aimed at the protection, sustainability, and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds
Authorizes the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to undertake or authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Nutrition
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural production, pricing, and nutrition education in schools.

Document 1: Amends the Agricultural Adjustment Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to base the minimum Class II milk price on the national average cost of production.
Directs the Secretary to identify all dairy products imported into and exported from the United States.
Document 2: Requires local educational agencies to include nutrition education in their school wellness policies.
Instruction must be consistent with the Dietary Guidelines for Americans.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to provide agricultural producers with commodity storage capacity loans.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative initiatives aimed at promoting renewable energy technologies and reducing greenhouse gas emissions in the transportation sector.

Document 1: repeal the Environmental Protection Agency's renewable fuel program
requires transportation fuel to contain a minimum volume of renewable fuel
Document 2: establish a competitive research, development, demonstration, and commercial application program
advance research and development and to demonstrate technologies, including plug-in hybrid technology
Document 3: produces at least 25 million gallons per year of liquid aviation turbine fuel
has at least 50% less lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions than petroleum

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting personally identifiable information and ensuring accountability for data breaches.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security.
Breach of security of any system maintained by a health care exchange established under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act.
Document 2: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as any information contained in a financial record.
Document 3: Imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to five years for intentionally or willfully concealing a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.

Makes any interstate business entity that collects, accesses, transmits, uses, stores, or disposes of sensitive personally identifiable information on 10,000 or more U.S. persons subject to the requirements for a data privacy and security program.

topic.name: Tax Fraud Prevention and Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on preventing tax fraud, identity theft, and ensuring fair audit practices by the IRS.

Document 1: provide a taxpayer who is selected for an audit sufficient information regarding the reason the taxpayer's return was selected for an audit
prohibit the IRS from conducting any audit primarily for research purposes.
Document 2: allow a conviction for money laundering based on merely concealing or disguising monetary instruments
expand the concept of monetary proceeds to include gross receipts.
Document 3: impose a criminal penalty on any person who willfully misappropriates another person's taxpayer identity
implement an identity theft tax fraud prevention program that provides for a unique personal identification number (PIN) on tax returns.

topic.name: Financial Regulation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating financial institutions and their operations.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA) to apply the bank share or asset acquisition requirements of the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to any company that is or would become an industrial bank holding company.
Subjects an industrial bank to Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) requirements regarding registration, reporting, and bank examinations.
Document 2: This bill amends the Federal Reserve Act to require the Chairman of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) to submit a Directive Policy Rule.
The GAO must also, upon congressional request, audit the conduct of monetary policy by the Board and the FOMC.
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to declare that no person shall be considered a fiduciary to an employee benefit plan by reason of performing any service as a swap dealer.
Prescribes criteria for considering a representative of a Special Entity to be independent of a swap dealer.

topic.name: Wildlife Regulation and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of nonnative wildlife and the conservation of marine mammals, specifically addressing the importation of wildlife parts.

Document 1: Invasive Fish and Wildlife Prevention Act of 2012
Requires FWS to determine whether the proposal should be approved within 180 days.
Document 2: Restoration of the U.S.-Russia Polar Bear Conservation Fund Act of 2013
Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to allow the importation of polar bear parts taken legally in sport hunts in Canada.
Document 3: Restoration of the U.S.-Russia Polar Bear Conservation Fund Act of 2015
Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to allow the importation of polar bear parts taken legally in sport hunts in Canada.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Measures
topic.description: The documents discuss various federal legislative measures, including the establishment of a working group for research efficiency and the designation of federal buildings.

Document 1: Requires the Director of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) to establish a working group under the authority of the National Science and Technology Council
Makes the working group responsible for reviewing federal regulations affecting research and research universities
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 522 North Central Avenue in Phoenix, Arizona, as the 'Ed Pastor Post Office'
Document 3: This bill designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 121 Spring Street S.E. in Gainesville, Georgia, as the 'Sidney Olsin Smith, Jr. Federal Building and United States Courthouse'

topic.name: Railroad Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety standards and risk management in railroad transportation, particularly concerning the transport of hazardous materials and the implementation of safety technologies.

Document 1: establish, by regulation, an interim national standard for the maximum volatility of crude oil transported by rail
DOT shall prohibit immediately the shipment of oil or ethanol in any DOT-111 tank car that does not meet the requirements
Document 2: extends from December 31, 2015, to December 31, 2020, the deadline for submission to the Department of Transportation

requires Class I railroad carriers and each entity providing regularly scheduled intercity or commuter rail passenger transportation to submit annual progress reports to DOT on the status of plans to implement a PTC system
Document 3: Allows the local matching share provided by a public transportation capital project assistance recipient to include expenditures by a private commuter vanpooling service provider
Authorizes such a private provider to use vanpool revenues exceeding operating costs to acquire such vans

topic.name: STEM Education and Engagement
topic.description: Focus on initiatives and grants aimed at increasing participation of underrepresented students in STEM fields.

Document 1: engages underrepresented students in grades kindergarten through 8 in STEM
encourage interest, engagement, and skills development of underrepresented students in STEM
Document 2: promote science, technology, engineering (including robotics), and mathematics (STEM) in secondary schools
broaden secondary school students' access to STEM careers

topic.name: Retirement and Health Savings Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing retirement savings options and health savings accounts for employees and small businesses.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to employer-established simple individual retirement accounts (IRAs) and pension plans
establish automatic deferral IRAs to permit the automatic enrollment of employees earning at least $5,000 in a preceding year
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income and Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) to exclude appraisers of employee stock ownership plans (ESOPs) from the meaning of a fiduciary subject to ERISA
Document 3: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act that limited payments for medications from health savings accounts
Rescinds appropriated but unobligated discretionary funds determined by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB)

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing support and resources to small businesses through grants, procurement programs, and financial assistance.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to develop and implement a grant program for state and local governmental and other nonprofit organizations located in the United States that support startup businesses.
Directs the SBA to condition receipt of a grant under this Act on the recipient obtaining matching funding for the same purposes from a local or state government grant and nonpublic funding.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act relating to the women's procurement program to require the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to wait until five years after the program is implemented before conducting a required study.

States that a contracting officer need not make a finding of past gender discrimination by a contracting agency in order to comply with federal contracting requirements under the women's business procurement program.

topic.name: Indian Tribal Land Settlements
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts related to the settlement of land claims and federal recognition of Indian tribes, focusing on the management and compensation of tribal lands and resources.

Document 1: Extinguishes all claims and all right, title, and interest in and to specified Indian and non-Indian land as part of the settlement of disputes within the Fort Hall Indian Reservation
Establishes a tribal trust fund account from which amounts... shall be distributed to the Tribes.
Document 2: Reaffirms federal recognition of the Dunlap Band of Mono Indians
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all programs, benefits, and services provided by the United States to Indians and Indian tribes.
Document 3: Amends the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe Infrastructure Development Trust Fund Act to increase the aggregate amount to be deposited into the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe Infrastructure Development Trust Fund

States that this Act shall be considered to be full and final compensation to the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe and the Crow Creek Sioux Tribe for damages caused by the construction of the Fort Randall Dam.

topic.name: Social Security Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at improving support and benefits for specific groups within the Social Security system.

1: Amends title II of the Social Security Act
apply a specified earnings test in the determination of the amount of monthly insurance benefits for blind beneficiaries
2: Amends part A of title IV of the Social Security Act
increase from 12 to 36 months the child's age limit for exemption by a state of single parents
3: Directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics to prepare and publish a monthly index
indicates monthly changes in expenditures for consumption that are typical for individuals 62 years or older

topic.name: Regulatory Review and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses the processes and requirements for the review, accountability, and transparency of federal agency regulations and rules.

Document 1: Requires each federal agency to publish in the Federal Register a plan for the periodic review of its major rules.
Requires the review to include a cost-benefit analysis of the rule.
Document 2: This bill amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to require the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to publish in the Federal Register a list of information on which the rule is based.
Congress must enact a joint resolution of approval of major rules before they may take effect.
Document 3: This bill requires a federal agency to include in each publication in the Federal Register pertaining to a rule the specific legal authority for the rule.

Each such officer, employee, or person shall, during the rule making period, file a report of each financial transaction required to be reported by a person under the Ethics in Government Act of 1978.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and developing diplomatic strategies to address issues related to Iran, Sudan, and Syria.

Document 1: U.S. policy to encourage foreign governments to cease all investment in Iran's energy sector
President is urged to impose sanctions on the Central Bank of Iran
Document 2: Directs the President to develop a comprehensive strategy to end serious human rights violations in Sudan
Impose specified sanctions on any person or government that supported serious human rights violations in Sudan
Document 3: Prohibits funds for supporting military operations in Syria
Directs the Secretary of State to report on assistance provided to groups in Syria

topic.name: Support for Homeless Populations
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at improving access to housing, healthcare, and education for homeless individuals and families.

Document 1: require hospitals to ensure patients are referred to appropriate medical care upon discharge and are placed in stable and appropriate housing
expand substance abuse and mental health services for the homeless
Document 2: require financial institutions to support housing opportunities for veterans
establishing housing opportunities or assisted living facilities for veterans
Document 3: raise the minimum allotment for each state for the education of homeless youth
ensure that homeless youth do not lose credits earned in other schools

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating campaign financing and ensuring fair elections in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA)
establishment of the Fair Elections Fund and of a Fair Elections Oversight Board
Document 2: Prohibits the use of federal funds received by any entity, other than a natural person, to advocate the election or defeat of a political candidate
Exempts from this ban the use of funds by an entity for an activity specifically authorized by federal law, rule, or regulation
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934
Requires public disclosure of the meeting, attendees, and matters discussed

topic.name: Consumer Credit Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at protecting consumers in credit transactions and ensuring fair practices in credit card operations.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit credit from being extended in any consumer credit transaction
if such consumer lacks a Social Security account number.
Document 2: Prohibits a creditor, during a specified nine-month period, from increasing any annual percentage rate (APR)
changing the terms governing repayment of any outstanding balance.
Document 3: Amends the Credit Card Accountability Responsibility and Disclosure Act of 2009
requires the issuer of such a certificate or card to comply with a specified prohibition against fees.

topic.name: Telecommunications Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation and provision of telecommunications services and broadcasting in the United States.

Document 1: Amends federal copyright law and the Communications Act of 1934
allow satellite carriers or cable operators to offer certain Colorado-based television broadcast stations
Document 2: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC)
revise its regulations to provide that digital audio radio satellite service (DARSS)
Document 3: Amends the Telecommunications Act of 1996

declare that the Federal Communications Commission's (FCC's) authority to take actions to accelerate deployment of advanced telecommunications capabilities shall not be construed to authorize the FCC to prevent states from implementing state laws

topic.name: Military Benefits and Healthcare
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing benefits and healthcare access for military personnel and their dependents.

Document 1: Transportation on DOD aircraft for active duty and reserve members
Establishes order of priority based on military needs
Document 2: Retirement eligibility age reduction for Ready Reserve members
Prohibits reduction below age 50 for retirement eligibility
Document 3: Eliminates residency requirement for medical care for dependents
Removes duty order condition for coverage eligibility

topic.name: Lead Safety Regulations
topic.description: The documents discuss regulations and amendments related to lead safety in drinking water and children's products.

Document 3: Consumer Product Safety Solutions Act of 2009 - Amends the Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act of 2008 to apply the limits on lead in children's products
Allows the CPSC to extend the application of, or exempt manufacturers, distributors, or retailers from, any provision of the Act or its amendments.

topic.name: Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at improving labor rights, workplace flexibility, and collective bargaining for employees.

Document 1: authorizes an employee to request from an employer a change in the terms or conditions of the employee's employment
makes it unlawful for an employer to interfere with any rights provided to an employee under this Act
Document 2: amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to authorize the government of Puerto Rico to enact law nullifying the application of the federal minimum wage
Document 3: requires a final decision of the VA with respect to the review of an adverse personnel action against a VA employee to be issued not later than 60 days
subjects such decision to judicial review in the appropriate U.S. District Court

topic.name: Detention and Transfer Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislation regarding the detention, transfer, and treatment of enemy combatants, particularly those held at Guantanamo Bay.

Document 1: Prohibits the transfer to, or release in, the United States of any unprivileged enemy belligerent
includes any individual (other than a member of the Armed Forces) under detention at United States Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba
Document 2: Prohibits federal funds from being used to transfer any enemy combatant detained at the Naval Station Guantanamo Bay, Cuba
Prohibits the Secretary of Defense from using any facility closed through a base closure process for housing any such individual

topic.name: Educational Support Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational resources and support services in under-resourced schools.

Document 1: Nurses for Under-Resourced Schools Everywhere Act
competitive matching demonstration grants to local educational agencies
Document 2: Supporting Community Schools Act of 2015
transformation schools identified as needing improvement
Document 3: Elementary and Secondary Education Amendments Act of 2011
intervention strategies for lowest performing schools

topic.name: Voting Rights and Election Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and amendments aimed at improving voter access, election administration, and redistricting processes in the United States.

Document 1: technical amendments to the U.S. Code with respect to title 52 (Voting and Elections)
voting accessibility for the elderly and handicapped
Document 2: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again
Requires such redistricting to be conducted in accordance with the plan developed and enacted into law by the independent redistricting commission
Document 3: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act (UOCAVA)
Prohibits states from refusing to accept balloting materials on any of specified grounds

topic.name: Gun Control Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at regulating firearms and addressing gun violence in the United States.

Document 1: expands resources for Project Gunrunner initiative
combat firearms trafficking and other criminal enterprises
Document 2: prohibit the transfer or possession of a large capacity ammunition feeding device
sets penalties for violations
Document 3: prohibits considering certain individuals as adjudicated mentally defective for firearms rights
administering laws and benefits by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs

topic.name: Aviation Security and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the security, modernization, and regulation of aviation operations in the United States.

Document 1: Airport Security Enhancement Act of 2007
designate certain airport areas as Federal Special Security Zones
Document 2: modernizing the C-130 aircraft fleet
ensure that all C-130 aircraft are capable of meeting applicable regulations of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
Document 3: Los Angeles Residential Helicopter Noise Relief Act of 2013
prescribe regulations for helicopter operations in Los Angeles County

topic.name: Food Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation, safety, and inspection of food products and facilities in the United States.

Document 1: expands the exemption of custom slaughtering of animals from federal inspection requirements
prepared exclusively for distribution to household consumers in the state
Document 2: deem genetically-engineered fish to be unsafe under provisions related to new animal drugs and adulterated food
Document 3: requires the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to establish a plan to ensure effective information sharing regarding the regulation and inspection of food products and facilities
issue a joint report to Congress summarizing the effectiveness or lack of effectiveness of this new information sharing arrangement

topic.name: U.S. Territorial and Minority Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislation focused on the application of immigration laws in U.S. territories and the recognition and support of minority groups, particularly Native Hawaiians and minority-serving institutions.

Document 1: effective date for applying U.S. immigration laws to the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to report to Congress
Document 2: defines terms, including 'qualified Native Hawaiian constituent'
establishes the U.S. Office for Native Hawaiian Relations
Document 3: establishes an Office of Minority Serving Institution Digital and Wireless Technology
requires the NSF Director to establish an advisory council

topic.name: Servicemembers' Rights Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting the rights and interests of servicemembers, particularly in relation to consumer credit and federal rule challenges.

Document 1: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to authorize a servicemember... to request that a consumer reporting agency... include in that servicemember's file a military service deployment alert.
Requires the CRA to provide the alert to each person who requests the credit score or consumer report of such servicemember.
Document 2: Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule... a legal brief challenging the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.
Document 3: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to allow the use of arbitration to resolve a controversy that arises under a contract with a servicemember...
Allows a servicemember's waiver of any of such Act's rights and protections to be effective only if it is made after a specific dispute has arisen.

topic.name: Rural Postal Service Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection and enhancement of postal services in rural areas of the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the United States Postal Service (USPS) from closing any post office which results in more than 10 miles distance between any 2 post offices.
Document 2: Imposes a two-year moratorium on the closing or consolidation of any postal facility.
Sets forth criteria for the closing or consolidation of rural post offices.
Establishes the Rural Postal Service Advisory Commission to provide strategic guidance on enhancing and protecting postal services in rural areas.

topic.name: Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing public safety through law enforcement protocols, protection of vulnerable populations, and combating counterfeit military goods.

Document 1: impose criminal penalties on persons who traffic in counterfeit goods or services
could reasonably be foreseen to cause serious bodily injury or death, disclosure of classified information
Document 2: set forth the due process rights, including procedures, that shall be afforded a law enforcement officer
requires law enforcement agencies to adopt and comply with a written complaint procedure
Document 3: authorize the Attorney General to award public safety and community policing grants
assist federal, state, tribal, and local law enforcement agencies in the rapid recovery of missing children and elderly and disabled individuals

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures related to abortion access, parental notification, and protections for healthcare entities involved in abortion services.

Document 1: prohibit any recipient of a grant or contract under the family planning program from discriminating against any institutional or individual health care entity
Defines 'health care entity' to include an individual physician or other health care professional
Document 2: Directs each state that makes a Medicaid payment from federal funds during the fiscal year for any items or services furnished by an abortion provider to report to the Secretary of Health and Human Services
Requires an annual report to specified congressional committees on such reports
Document 3: Prohibits any person or organization in or affecting interstate or foreign commerce from performing, permitting facilities to be used to perform, or assisting in the performance of an abortion on an unemancipated minor
Prescribes penalties of not more than a $100,000 fine and/or imprisonment for not more than one year for each willful violation

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Safety and Health
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing safety and health practices in various sectors, including wildland firefighting and school health education.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior... to jointly submit annual reports to Congress on the wildland firefighter safety practices
Requires such reports to include: progress made in implementing recommendations from the Inspector General of the Department of Agriculture
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services... to develop and disseminate to school administrators, educators, school health professionals
guidelines regarding the placement of automated external defibrillators in schools and child care centers

topic.name: Sexual Assault Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to legal and medical support for victims of sexual assault, particularly within military and educational contexts.

Document 1: Expands eligibility for legal assistance provided by the Armed Forces special victims' counsel for eligible members and dependents who are victims of sex-related offenses
a member of a reserve component who is a victim of an alleged sex-offense during...
Document 2: authorizes the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants for state surveys concerning health care provider access for sexual assault forensic examination services
Each institution of higher education that receives federal funds must publish its plan for ensuring access to sexual assault medical forensic examinations and treatments.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to review the adequacy of the training, qualifications, and experience of each member of the Armed Forces and civilian DOD employee who is currently responsible for sexual assault prevention and response
to prescribe in regulations: appropriate minimum levels of training, qualifications, and experience for such personnel.

topic.name: District of Columbia Legislative Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes aimed at enhancing the governance and representation of the District of Columbia, including autonomy, representation, and educational funding reforms.

Document 1: eliminate congressional review of newly-passed District laws
Document 2: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives and in the Senate
Document 3: requires the District of Columbia to provide education savings accounts for children eligible for free public education

topic.name: U.S. Trade Enforcement Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enforcing U.S. trade laws and preventing trade evasion.

Document 1: Preventing Recurring Trade Evasion and Circumvention Act or PROTECT Act
establish within the Office of International Trade of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection a Trade Remedy Law Enforcement Division
Document 2: Customs Trade Facilitation and Enforcement Act of 2012
establishes U.S. Customs and Border Protection as the U.S. Customs and Border Protection Agency

topic.name: Health and Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing health issues and promoting social welfare through targeted assistance and community involvement.

Document 1: addresses the social and health issues that lead to obstetric fistula
support treatment of obstetric fistula
Document 2: conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities
reduce HIV/AIDS in the African-American community
Document 3: financial assistance, technology transfers, or capacity building assistance to reduce deforestation
promote local participation in the design, implementation, and independent monitoring of policies and initiatives

topic.name: Family Caregiver Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing support, leave, and financial assistance to family caregivers, particularly in the context of military service and family transitions.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to furnish advanced instruction and training and certification as a family caregiver for a veteran.
Requires the Secretary to provide appropriate support services and a monthly family caregiver allowance.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Labor to make five-year grants to assist families by providing wage replacement for caregiving needs.
Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act to allow additional leave for parental involvement and family medical care needs.
Document 3: Entitles an employed family member of a member of the Armed Forces to two workweeks of leave per year for each family member deployed.
Provides employment and benefits protection for employees upon their return from such leave.

topic.name: U.S. Bond Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the issuance, regulation, and benefits of various types of bonds in the United States.

Document 1: directs the Department of the Treasury to allow an individual taxpayer to use a portion or all of a tax refund to purchase U.S. savings bonds
an option for a tax return that allows for the gifting of such bonds
Document 2: make permanent the issuance authority for Build America Bonds
allow refundings of currently issued bonds
Document 3: extends until January 1, 2013, the period for issuing Build America Bonds
allows funding through such bonds for capital expenditures for levees and flood control projects

topic.name: Anti-Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at prohibiting discrimination based on various identities in educational and healthcare settings.

Document 1: Prohibits public school students from being excluded from participating in... on the basis of their actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity.
Considers harassment to be a form of discrimination.
Document 2: This bill amends title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 to define 'sex' as the biological sex of an individual for purposes of enforcing the prohibition against discrimination on the basis of sex.
Prohibits discrimination on the basis of sex under education programs or activities receiving federal financial assistance.
Document 3: Prohibits discrimination on the basis of sex, race, color, national origin, sexual orientation, or disability status against any person in the United States.
Under any health care service or research program or activity receiving federal financial assistance.

topic.name: Cultural and Environmental Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the management and preservation of cultural sites and environmental resources through legislative actions.

Document 1: mandate that the courses be operated and maintained in a manner that retains the historic nature of the courses
ensures that at least two of the courses maintain fees related to use that are affordable
Document 2: establish the Hudson-Mohawk River Basin Commission to coordinate activities
develop and implement plans, policies, and projects relating to the Basin's water resources
Document 3: authorize the World War I Memorial Foundation to establish a commemorative work rededicating the District of Columbia War Memorial

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments for Industries
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting various industries, including distilled spirits, alternative fuels, and medical devices.

Document 1: eliminate the drawback fee on the manufacture or production of distilled spirits
used in non-beverage products
Document 2: extend through 2010 the excise tax credit for alternative fuels
involving liquified petroleum gas
Document 3: repeal the excise tax on medical device manufacturers
amends the Internal Revenue Code

topic.name: U.S. Policy on Taiwan and North Korea
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative actions and strategies regarding Taiwan's defense and international relations, as well as humanitarian efforts related to North Korean refugees.

Document 1: Establishes the Congressional Advisory Commission on the Implementation of United States Policy under the Taiwan Relations Act.
Recommend policy options to assist Taiwan broaden its international space.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of State to develop a comprehensive strategy for facilitating the adoption of North Korean children by U.S. citizens.
Document 3: A critical element to maintaining peace and stability in Asia is ensuring a militarily strong Taiwan.
The United States has a statutory obligation under the Taiwan Relations Act to help Taiwan maintain sufficient self-defense capabilities.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving public health through preventive medicine, immunization, and coordination of health initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for training in preventive medicine specialties.
Requires grant funds to be used for residency programs for preventive medicine or public health.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish an Autoimmune Diseases Interdepartmental Coordinating Committee.
Develop criteria to be used in defining and identifying autoimmune diseases.
Document 3: Includes immunizations against infectious diseases within authorized preventive health services available to veterans.
Requires the inclusion in an annual report on VA preventive health services of programs to ensure veterans receive immunizations.

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Specific Issues
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing specific regulatory and economic issues in healthcare, telecommunications, and environmental policy.

Document 1: Preserving Access to Orphan Drugs Act of 2013
excludes any drug or biological product which is approved or licensed by the FDA for marketing solely for one or more rare diseases
Document 2: Wireless Tax Fairness Act of 2015
prohibits states or local governments from imposing any new discriminatory tax on mobile services
Document 3: Agency PAYGO for Greenhouse Gases Act
requires the EPA to include an offset in any proposed rule that limits greenhouse gas emissions

topic.name: Regulatory Amendments in Safety and Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments aimed at enhancing safety and health regulations across different sectors, including energy, mining, and transportation.

Document 1: This bill amends the Natural Gas Act to require that civil penalties for violations of the Act be assessed by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
guidelines which it apply regarding civil penalties for violations under the Federal Power Act.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that essential standards to protect miner health are out of date

Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health (NIOSH) to forward to the Secretary of Labor its Recommended Exposure Limits for chemical and other hazards to miners.
Document 3: Revises specified administrative requirements for the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB)
Expands the authority of the NTSB to investigate accidents which result in the death of or serious injury to a person.

topic.name: Economic and Environmental Policy
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing economic safety nets and environmental standards.

Document 1: amends the Clean Air Act
require the review of national ambient air quality standards
Document 2: Amends the Federal-State Extended Unemployment Compensation Act
revise the formula for 'on' and 'off' indicators
Document 3: provides for federal-state agreements for increased regular unemployment compensation payments
requires federal payments to states to cover 100% of such additional payments

topic.name: Gambling Legislation and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures concerning the regulation and prohibition of gambling activities, including internet gambling and sports broadcasting rights.

Document 1: prohibit the use of gambling devices on property under the jurisdiction of the Department of Defense
except for charitable events and state lotteries.
Document 2: deny the antitrust exemption for joint agreements covering the telecasting of sports contests
make a sponsored telecast of a game that is played in the home territory of a member club available to consumers
Document 3: prohibition against transmission of wagering information shall apply to any bet or wager
making such prohibition applicable to all types of gambling activities, including internet gambling

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Assistance and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at providing assistance and security support to nations involved in conflict, particularly focusing on Iraq and Afghanistan.

Document 1: increases the number of Iraqi and Afghan translators working directly with U.S. Armed Forces who may be provided special immigrant status
Document 2: Authorizes the President to provide assistance for Pakistan to support: democratic institutions, economic development, health care, and human rights
Expresses the sense of Congress in favor of efforts to assist refugees and displaced persons in Pakistan
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of State to ensure that only government personnel provide security services at U.S. diplomatic or consular missions in Iraq or Afghanistan
Requires the President to report to Congress on the status of planning for the use of government and military personnel instead of private contractors in conflict zones

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. foreign policy actions and sanctions related to nuclear cooperation with Saudi Arabia and financial transactions with Cuba.

Document 1: Affirms the strong and historic ties between the United States and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

Disapproves of the May 2008 Memorandum of Understanding between the government of the United States of America and the government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Concerning Cooperation in Nuclear Energy.
Document 2: This bill prohibits a U.S. person from engaging in any financial transaction with or transfer of funds to the Ministry of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Cuba.
A person that violates or attempts to violate such prohibitions shall be subject to specified penalties under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.
Document 3: Prohibits Department of Commerce funds from being used to implement, administer, or enforce specified amendments to Department regulations restricting gift parcels and humanitarian donations to Cuba.
The amendments prohibit the sending of gifts of clothing, personal hygiene items, seeds, fishing equipment, soap-making equipment, and veterinary medicine and supplies.

topic.name: Census and Population Counting
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies related to accurate population counting in the census, including considerations for specific demographics and military personnel.

Document 1: include in any questionnaire used in a decennial census to determine state populations an option for respondents to indicate Dominican extraction or descent
Document 2: ensure that all members of the Armed Forces deployed abroad are fully and accurately counted in the tabulation of total population by states

topic.name: Mining Legislation and Reclamation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating mining practices, ensuring the health benefits of miners, and addressing environmental remediation of mining sites.

Document 1: Miners Protection Act of 2015
amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977
Document 2: Three Kids Mine Remediation and Reclamation Act
environmental remediation and reclamation of the Site
Document 3: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977
allocation of surface mining reclamation funds to states and Indian tribes

topic.name: Health and Compensation for Exposed Workers
topic.description: Legislation addressing health care and compensation for workers exposed to radiation and hazardous conditions during their employment.

Document 1: Santa Susana Fair Compensation Act
compensation or benefits under such Act
Document 2: Atomic Veterans Healthcare Parity Act
radiation exposed veterans
Document 3: Public Health Service Act
employer-based wellness programs

topic.name: Government Accountability and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving accountability, service delivery, and training within various government agencies and programs.

Document 1: amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act to allow the Department of Health and Human Services to remove, transfer, or demote certain employees of the Indian Health Service based on performance or misconduct
establish requirements for IHS and its employees with respect to retaliation and other conduct
Document 2: directs the Federal Emergency Management Agency to establish the RESPONSE Subcommittee to develop recommendations for improving emergency responder training
the availability and effectiveness of federal, state, local, and nongovernmental funding levels related to training emergency responders for rail hazmat incidents
Document 3: authorizes and directs the Secretary of the Treasury to continue to operate the Internal Revenue Service Free File program
provides free online individual income tax preparation and electronic filing services to lower income taxpayers

topic.name: Federal Court Reorganization and Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative actions related to the restructuring of federal courts and the management of federal lands.

Document 1: Divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into new circuits
Directs the President to appoint additional judges for the circuits.
Document 2: Establishes a pilot program in U.S. district courts for patent cases
Cases are randomly assigned to district court judges.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to offer for disposal certain federal lands
Previously identified as suitable for disposal in a report to Congress.

topic.name: Transportation and Tax Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to transportation regulations and tax incentives affecting fuel economy, charitable contributions, and vehicle disclosures.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the alcohol fuels tax credit and the alternative fuel tax credit
Amends federal transportation law to require manufacturers of new automobiles that can operate on gasoline or diesel fuel to manufacture such automobiles
Document 2: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individuals, trusts, or estates to exclude from gross income up to $50 of interest and money market fund dividend income that is contributed to charity
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to require all insurers and self-insurers of passenger motor vehicles to disclose to the public
the date of and primary reason for the total loss determination

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions on War and Justice
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures related to war accountability, employment services, and military operations.

Document 3: Prohibits the obligation or expenditure of funds for the purpose of supporting military or paramilitary operations in Syria, except after a formal declaration of war.
Directs the Secretary of State to report every 90 days on assistance provided to groups, organizations, movements, and individuals in Syria.

topic.name: Weather Forecasting and Disaster Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing weather forecasting capabilities and improving disaster preparedness and response.

Document 1: directs the National Weather Service (NWS) to employ at each of its weather forecast offices at least one warning coordination meteorologist
work closely with state, local, and tribal emergency management agencies to ensure a planned, coordinated, and effective preparedness effort.
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to carry out a program for an improved ocean surface winds vector satellite
to improve forecasting for hurricanes, coastal winds and storm surge, and other weather-related disasters.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Army to review operation of such reservoir using improved weather forecasts and run-off forecasting methods
determine if a change in operations will improve core functions of the Army Corps of Engineers, including reducing risks to human life, public safety, property.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts aimed at addressing specific operational, funding, and land entitlement issues.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Fire Prevention and Control Act of 1974 to authorize the Administrator of the United States Fire Administration to award up to 100 grants for task forces
Requires grantees to obtain non-federal matching funds of at least 55% of the amount awarded by the Administrator for each fiscal year.
Document 2: Establishes the Bipartisan Earmark Reform Commission to develop a clear definition of what constitutes an earmark
Requires the Majority Leaders of the House of Representatives and of the Senate to introduce the legislation recommended in the report.
Document 3: Gives Sealaska, the regional Alaska Native Corporation for southeast Alaska, 90 days following this Act's enactment to agree to accept the conveyance of specified federal land
Identifies the lands to be conveyed upon Sealaska's agreement as 25 parcels of federal land comprising approximately 69,235 acres.

topic.name: Federal Education and Housing Policy
topic.description: Legislation addressing federal funding and policy reforms related to education grants and housing assistance programs.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to make the formula for calculation of an individual Pell Grant inapplicable for the 2007-2008 academic year.
suspends for such year the 'tuition sensitivity provision' which prohibits maximum Pell Grant awards to students attending low-tuition institutions.
Document 2: Rescinds and cancels permanently all unobligated balances for the redevelopment of abandoned and foreclosed homes.
Urges that all such rescinded amounts be retained in the Treasury General Fund for reducing the federal budget deficit.
Document 3: Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to determine for each county with a population exceeding one million individuals whether a significant share resides in sub-areas with high median prices.
Authorizes the Director to increase the limitation on the maximum original principal obligation of a mortgage.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Insurance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to financial institutions, including deposit insurance and innovative savings programs.

Document 1: temporary increase from $100,000 to $250,000 in the maximum insured amount limitation for deposit insurance
establish a temporary increase in FDIC and Credit Union Insurance Act
Document 2: authorize covered financial institutions to conduct a contest, known as a 'savings promotion raffle'
exempt savings promotion raffles conducted by an insured depository institution from specified prohibitions

topic.name: Tax Legislation and Benefits
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures related to tax benefits, deductions, and credits for homeowners, adoption expenses, and health insurance providers.

Document 1: extend through 2015 the exclusion from gross income of income attributable to the discharge of indebtedness on a principal residence
extend through 2014 the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums
Document 2: repeals a provision of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act that imposes an annual fee on a health insurance provider based on its net premium income
Document 3: makes permanent provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 allowing an increased tax credit for adoption expenses
increased exclusion from gross income for amounts paid by an employer for adoption expenses

topic.name: Government Regulations on Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating various services provided by government agencies, including file sharing, veterans' burial services, and inmate communication services.

Document 1: prohibit the download, installation, or use by government employees and contractors of open-network peer-to-peer file sharing software
develop a procedure for receiving requests from heads or chief information officers of agencies for approval
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to furnish a casket or urn of sufficient quality for a dignified burial
report to the congressional veterans committees on VA compliance with industry standards for caskets and urns
Document 3: direct the Federal Communications Commission to adopt regulations on the use of inmate telephone and video services
prescribe a maximum uniform per-minute rate and service connection or other per-call rate

topic.name: Regulatory Frameworks in Health and Insurance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating education, licensing, and health services related to the insurance and healthcare sectors.

Document 1: prohibit the courses offered by lenders for their own employees from satisfying the pre-licensing education or continuing education requirement.
Document 2: reestablishes the NARAB without contingent conditions as a nonprofit corporation to prescribe, on a multi-state basis, licensing and insurance producer qualification requirements
retains states' regulatory authority over: licensing, supervision, disciplining, and setting of licensing fees for insurance producers.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation and policies governing oil and gas exploration and production on federal and state lands.

Document 1: prohibit Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil or natural gas
off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington
Document 2: addresses oil and gas production on federal lands in Alaska
Document 3: coordinate permitting activities by federal and state agencies regarding any oil and gas project under a federal lease
ensure the compliance of participating federal and state agencies

topic.name: Health and Education Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on health care regulations and educational funding reforms.

Document 1: Requires the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to inspect, at least once every two years, each establishment that engages in the manufacture of human cells, tissues, and cellular and tissue-based products.
Sets forth penalties for failing to comply with model form requirements or for knowingly using fraudulent information.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make enhanced matching payments to states that provide incentive payments to physicians, hospitals, and community health centers that exhibit meaningful use of certified health information technology.

Limits such payments to each state that establishes a program to design, develop, install, maintain, and operate electronic information and eligibility systems and patient registries for the purpose of disease screening.

topic.name: Regulation of Pharmaceutical Marketing
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating the marketing and dissemination of information related to pharmaceuticals and medical products.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to deny a business expense tax deduction for expenses relating to advertising or promoting the sale and use of prescription pharmaceuticals.
Document 2: Requires a person who owns or operates a retail establishment and uses mobile device tracking technology in the establishment to display in a prominent location a notice that such technology is in use.
Document 3: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to review each regulation and guidance that applies to the dissemination by means of the Internet of information about medical products.

topic.name: Safety Legislation for Children
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing the safety and protection of children in various environments, including home and school.

Document 1: Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring warning labels on baby monitors
alert users that video and audio from such devices may be intercepted and viewed or heard by potential intruders
Document 2: Requires local educational agencies to have in effect policies that deem to be reasonable and permissible a search of any minor student
to ensure that the school and students remain free from the threat of all weapons, dangerous materials, or illegal narcotics
Document 3: Establishes an interim standard for equestrian helmets manufactured nine months after enactment of this Act
Directs the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to begin rulemaking proceedings for development of a final standard.

topic.name: Government Policy and Management
topic.description: The documents discuss various government policies related to land management, veterans' education, and labor statistics improvement.

Document 1: Establishes the Clear Creek National Recreation Area in California to promote environmentally responsible high-quality motorized and non-motorized trail based recreation
Requires the Secretary to create a comprehensive management plan for the Recreation Area within two years of enactment of this Act.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to enter into a contract with a non-government entity to conduct a survey of a statistically valid sample of individuals
Requires the survey to be designed to collect specified types of information about each individual surveyed.
Document 3: Establishes an independent Commission to Improve Labor Statistics
Directs the Commission to examine and assess the process by which the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor collects, processes, analyzes, and disseminates statistical data.

topic.name: Health and Insurance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to health insurance coverage and requirements for insurers to disclose information to consumers.

Document 1: Flood Insurance Disclosure Act of 2007
requires insurers to disclose...
Document 2: allows individuals to enroll in health insurance...
coverage of such treatment by such provider
Document 3: Ensuring Quality Health Care for All Americans Act of 2012
repeals the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act

topic.name: Federal Legislative Actions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various federal legislative measures aimed at authorizing specific actions related to military, education, and infrastructure projects.

Document 1: Amends the Military Construction Authorization Act, 1974 to allow portions of the former bombardment area on the island of Culebra, Puerto Rico, to be utilized for purposes that would require decontamination at the expense of the United States.
Makes the authority for such utilization applicable to portions of such island identified as having regular public access in the Department of Defense (DOD) study.
Document 2: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to the Hope Scholarship tax credit to include room, board, fees, books, supplies, and equipment as expenses eligible for such credit.
Makes the Hope Scholarship and Lifetime Learning tax credits refundable.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, to establish a road corridor following 1 of the 2 routes identified as 'Niblack Route 1' or 'Niblack Route 2'.
Expresses the intent of Congress that any federal permit required for such road construction be issued or denied within one year after the date of the permit application.

topic.name: Environmental Protection and Research
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to the protection of marine environments and the establishment of agricultural research institutions.

Document 1: protect and preserve living and other resources of the Gulf of the Farallones National Marine Sanctuary
prohibits the issuance of a lease or permit authorizing mineral or hydrocarbon exploration
Document 2: establishes within the Department of Agriculture the National Institute of Food and Agriculture
provides grants to support and promote the highest quality of fundamental agricultural research

topic.name: Energy and Economic Development Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at enhancing energy security and economic development through collaborative programs and accountability measures.

Document 1: Military Energy Security Act
collaborative energy security pilot program
Document 2: Unauthorized Spending Accountability Act
establishes a three-year reauthorization, rescission, and termination schedule
Document 3: Cluster-Based Economy Enhancement Act
authorize the Secretary of Commerce to make grants to assess the potential for the development or enhancement of business clusters

topic.name: U.S. Fisheries and Trade Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations regarding the conservation of fish species, the management of exclusive economic zones, and the facilitation of trade in natural resources.

Document 1: Prohibits any person from offering billfish or billfish products for sale
Subjects a person to a maximum civil penalty of $100,000 for each violation
Document 2: Designates the U.S. exclusive economic zone as the 'Ronald Wilson Reagan Exclusive Economic Zone of the United States'
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to treat each NATO member nation as a nation with which there is in effect a free trade agreement

topic.name: Legislation on Health and Rights
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to health issues and the protection of individual rights.

Document 1: Amends the federal judicial code to provide that no court shall have jurisdiction to hear or decide a question of state pornography law.
A decision of a federal court is not binding precedent on a state court.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the National Cancer Institute to establish Ovarian Cancer Biomarker Centers of Excellence.
Permits federal funds to be used for research on the development and characterization of new biomarkers for ovarian cancer.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the increasing rate of suicide among veterans is a serious problem.
Directs the Secretary to conduct mandatory, face-to-face, and confidential mental health screenings for members of the Armed Forces.

topic.name: Infrastructure Investment Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on funding and improving infrastructure projects in the United States, including water systems, ferry services, and flood protection.

Document 1: Provides for the payment of not more than 35% of the cost of the Arkansas Valley Conduit
Requires appropriations for the construction of the Conduit.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation for FY2010-FY2015 for construction of ferry vessels and ferry terminal facilities.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish within the Department of Transportation a Ferry Joint Program Office.
Document 3: Modifies the project for flood damage reduction, American and Sacramento Rivers, California, to authorize improvements to flood damage reduction facilities.
Requires the nonfederal interests to receive credit for expenses incurred, or in-kind contributions provided.

topic.name: Health and Education Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving health and education services in the United States.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
makes those students eligible for interest subsidies
Document 2: amends the child and adult care food program of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act
require subsidized food served to children in child care centers and day care homes to meet the most recent Dietary Guidelines for Americans
Document 3: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy
ensure that Medicare beneficiaries have timely and appropriate access to infusion therapy in their homes

topic.name: Social Policy and Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at addressing social welfare, employment support, and regulatory frameworks for the care of nonhuman primates.

Document 1: Amends part D of title IV of the Social Security Act to condition continued approval of a state plan upon state assessment of its policies with respect to barriers to employment and financial support of children.

Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to states for an employment demonstration project involving a court- or state child support agency supervised-employment program for noncustodial parents.
